varieties.name: Melissa officinalis (Common/Standard)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean Basin, South-central Europe, Central Asia, Iran. Cultivated since 16th century.
varieties.traits: Bushy upright growth 50-90cm, green heart-shaped leaves with scalloped edges, heavily veined and hairy texture, square stems
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, multiple harvests per season, vigorous regrowth after cutting
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Queensland - Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (humid subtropical climates), Mudbrick Herb Cottage, Bunnings, Diggers Club, Seeds of Plenty, The Seed Collection, Happy Valley Seeds. Online and retail nurseries stock plants and seeds.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Citronella'
varieties.traits: 25-30cm height, compact bushy form, bright green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good, compact size ideal for containers
varieties.availability: Baker Creek Heirloom Seeds (online), specialty herb nurseries, occasionally at Queensland garden centers

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Aurea' (Golden Lemon Balm)
varieties.traits: 45-75cm height, compact growth, crinkly oval leaves with golden-yellow variegation and green splashing, especially vibrant in spring/fall
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, primarily ornamental but fully edible
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries Queensland, Australian native plant suppliers, online herb retailers. Sold as plants rather than seeds (doesn't come true from seed).

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'All Gold'
varieties.traits: 40-60cm height, solid bright golden-yellow to gold-green leaves (not variegated like 'Aurea'), neat domed clumps
varieties.availability: Australian herb specialists, online plant retailers, occasionally larger Queensland nurseries. Plants only (propagated vegetatively).

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Variegata' (Variegated Lemon Balm)
varieties.traits: 30-45cm height, dwarf compact form, yellow-green patterned leaves with irregular variegation
varieties.availability: Herb specialists Queensland, container herb collections, online retailers. Propagated vegetatively - plants only.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Quedlinburger Niederliegende'
varieties.origin: Quedlinburg, Germany (north of Harz mountains, Saxony-Anhalt). Traditional German breeding for essential oil production. Name means 'procumbent' (low-growing) though observed to be upright.
varieties.traits: 50-80cm height, broad wrinkled leaves more rigid than standard, stronger structure, bushy habit
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, most valuable for commercial essential oil cultivation
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers (Adaptive Seeds USA ships internationally), heirloom herb specialists, organic seed companies. Seeds and occasional plants.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis subsp. altissima 'Lime' (Lime Balm)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean subspecies, closely related to standard lemon balm
varieties.traits: 60-90cm height, brighter green foliage than lemon balm, slightly larger leaves, yellow-tinted growth
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, drought-tolerant unlike other varieties
varieties.availability: Australian herb nurseries (limited), online herb specialists, UK suppliers ship internationally (First Tunnels UK). Seeds and plants.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Compacta' (Dwarf Lemon Balm)
varieties.origin: Selected compact cultivar for container growing
varieties.traits: 15-40cm height (dwarf), very compact bushy form, dark green leaves, sterile (doesn't produce viable seed)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate for size, continuous harvest from compact plant
varieties.availability: Specialty compact herb collections, Outsidepride (dwarf varieties), Australian container herb suppliers. Plants only (sterile, must be propagated by division).

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Lemonella'
varieties.traits: 50-75cm height, uniform growth habit, consistent leaf size
varieties.availability: Commercial herb suppliers, large-scale nurseries, occasionally Australian agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Mandarina'
varieties.origin: Selected cultivar with unique citrus profile
varieties.traits: 40-70cm height, lower spreading growth habit than standard, broader stance
varieties.yield_notes: Good, spreading habit provides abundant foliage
varieties.availability: Specialty herb nurseries UK (First Tunnels), rare in Australia, occasional imports, herb collectors.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Binsuga'
varieties.origin: Modern selection bred for high essential oil content
varieties.traits: 60-90cm height, very vigorous bushy growth, robust structure
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent, especially vigorous growth for tea production
varieties.availability: European herb seed companies, specialty suppliers, rare in Australia, online international herb seed catalogs.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis (Common/Standard)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean Basin, South-central Europe, Central Asia, Iran. Cultivated since 16th century.
varieties.traits: Bushy upright growth 50-90cm, green heart-shaped leaves with scalloped edges, heavily veined and hairy texture, square stems
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, multiple harvests per season, vigorous regrowth after cutting
varieties.availability: OrganicBazar.net (India-wide delivery), The Affordable Organic Store (online India), Nurserylive.com (pan-India), Cleantech Mart, tropical monsoon climates local nurseries in Kottayam and Ernakulam districts, herbal farms in Idukki.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Citronella'
varieties.traits: 25-30cm height, compact bushy form, bright green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good, compact size ideal for containers
varieties.availability: Limited in India, international online seed companies (Baker Creek ships to India), specialty importers, herb collectors networks.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Compacta' (Dwarf Lemon Balm)
varieties.origin: Selected compact cultivar for container growing
varieties.traits: 15-40cm height (dwarf), very compact bushy form, dark green leaves, sterile (doesn't produce viable seed)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate for size, continuous harvest from compact plant
varieties.availability: Limited in India, specialty herb importers, international suppliers, propagated by division only.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Quedlinburger Niederliegende'

varieties.traits: 50-80cm height, broad wrinkled leaves more rigid than standard, stronger structure, bushy habit
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, most valuable for commercial essential oil cultivation

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis subsp. altissima 'Lime' (Lime Balm)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean subspecies, closely related to standard lemon balm
varieties.traits: 60-90cm height, brighter green foliage than lemon balm, slightly larger leaves, yellow-tinted growth
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, drought-tolerant unlike other varieties
varieties.availability: Rare in India, international herb seed companies, specialty importers, tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities may have research stock.

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Aurea' (Golden Lemon Balm)
varieties.traits: 45-75cm height, compact growth, crinkly oval leaves with golden-yellow variegation and green splashing
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, primarily ornamental but fully edible

varieties.name: Melissa officinalis 'Variegata' (Variegated Lemon Balm)
varieties.traits: 30-45cm height, dwarf compact form, yellow-green patterned leaves with irregular variegation
varieties.availability: Rare in India, specialty container herb collections, international suppliers, vegetatively propagated only.
